title: Usability cleanup for 'inspect'
body:
Make the usage messages (and options) different between
podman inspect, podman image inspect, and podman container inspect.

Disable inapplicable options (-l, -s) for podman image inspect

Disable -t (type) when the type is implicit through the subcommand.

Uglier than desirable due to Go and Cobra limitations

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Quick way to review output:
```
$ for i in '' image container; do podman $i inspect --help;echo ------------------------------;done
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago Seems like these changes are causing issues in tests.
@edsantiago If you make this change, please update the shared man page to indicate the three different ways it is called.
@edsantiago I was thinking that the verification of the pulling might be doing an inspect.  But we will see if they fail again.

Trying to figure out tests. Ginkgo no longer works on my test setup, not since it got vendored in:
```
test/e2e/libpod_suite_test.go:45:34: cannot use session (type *"github.com/onsi/gomega/gexec".Session) as type *"github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/onsi/gomega/gexec".Session in field value
```
I probably won't have time to work on this until tomorrow due to the Pipeline review.
New force push; there was indeed an erroneous `podman container inspect IMAGE` in ` commit_test.go`. I still don't understand the errors in `stop` and `kill` tests.
@edsantiago CI should be fixed, rebase please
